free_text: Stellar neutron capture cross sections of the
Experimental details. Pr and Dy experiment.
Total cross sections and related uncertainty
pulse width is less than 1 ns, repetition rate
Metallic Li target of about 1 mg/cm2
Proton beam energy adjusted so to be 30 and 100 keV
giving neutrons in energy ranges 3-100, 3-225 keV.
Standard sample Au-197, 15 mm diameter, 0.4 mm =
enclosing aluminum canning 0.2662 g for neutron flux
aluminum canning 0.3058 g , served for simulating the
Empty aluminum canning 0.2422 gram for sample
LI-6 glass detector (first), located close to
neutron target. Measured spectra of all samples were
normalized to equal neutron flux by means of Li-6 glass
Corrections were made for multiple scattering,
for isotopic impurities, for neutron self-absorption,
for undetected events, for signal/background ratio -
Error due to flight path uncertainty.
Error due to neutron flux normalization.
Error due to sample mass and elemental

according to Table V of main reference.
For quasi-metastable states "-M" was changed into "-L"
in according to new rule introduced.
replaced by KT column from Table IX of main reference;
